reasoning: Q1 achieved semantic correctness in iter_1 (4 correct rows produced), but regressed in execution time to 120ms while previous iteration had 90ms baseline. Critical floating-point precision errors (±0.01) must be fixed with Kahan summation. With 1 remaining iteration and no performance degradation expected, aggressively optimize using SIMD vectorization (1.5-2x speedup potential) and improve date filter accuracy to achieve competitive performance (<100ms target).
focus_areas: Implement Kahan summation for floating-point precision (critical semantic fix)
SIMD vectorization of filter+aggregation loop using AVX-512 (1.5-2x speedup expected, CPU-bound optimization)
Refine date filter threshold calculation (compute exact 1998-09-02 instead of hardcoded 10500)
strategy_notes: Iteration 1 fixed correctness but regressed performance. This iteration must: (1) preserve correctness with Kahan summation compensation, (2) recover performance with SIMD vectorization of the hot filter+aggregation loop. The morsel-driven parallel approach (32-48 threads) is good; focus SIMD gains on per-thread loops. AVX-512 available on hardware (lscpu shows avx512f, avx512dq). Process 8 int32 values per iteration for filter, 4 double accumulators per group. Target: 80-100ms execution time (competitive with DuckDB 86ms minimum). Avoid repeating iter_1's approach; ensure SIMD implementation doesn't add synchronization overhead.
notes: Both critical_fixes from iter_0 evaluation (Kahan summation + date filter accuracy) are high-priority. SIMD vectorization is proven technique for this workload pattern (filter + aggregation on sorted/sequential data). No correctness regression expected if Kahan summation is correctly implemented. This is the final iteration—be aggressive on performance optimization.
